title: Zotero upstream drift watch (dev radar)
goal: Track Zotero dev (main) drift in watched anchors. Released/beta versions are NOT yet affected; do not change release-targeting product code -- record where relocated logic moved for future adaptation.
resolution: deep-verify: all 4 dev-drifted targets hold on release (9.0.5), beta (9.0, byte-identical to release), and dev (main/40cb1cf); no product change. Observed additive dev-only refactors (informational, tracked in watchlist cascade_hints): collection-tree-render-item adds first/last-selected/focused class toggles (DOM selectors intact); collection-tree-row caching moved to per-instance _cachedSearch/_cachedResults/_cachedTempTable + clearCache(), onUnload temp-table DROP now guarded by if(!options.unfiltered) (lifecycle intact); item-save-data adds env.undoData accessDate snapshot (notifierData/_changed/parentID contracts intact); merge-items adds Zotero.UndoHistory.stageAction (signature + side effects unchanged). Also widened contract coverage: added contracts to item-save-data and collection-tree-render-item which previously had none.
in_scope: Inspect changed upstream watch targets: collection-tree-render-item, collection-tree-row-duplicates-search-object, item-save-data, merge-items
Verify the behavioral contracts of each changed target on the release tier.
Update Zoplicate compatibility code and tests only where a release/beta contract actually broke.
Refresh upstream contract snapshots after compatibility work.
out_of_scope: Broad refactors not required by upstream compatibility
Chasing dev-only (radar) drift with product code changes
Changing release metadata unless Zotero compatibility bounds actually change
acceptance: Review .workflow/upstream/zotero_upstream_report.md and classify every changed target by tier severity (urgent/scheduled/radar).

Verify collection-tree-render-item (radar) contracts still hold on the release tier: collectionTree renderItem builds a row whose primary cell is a 'span.cell.label.primary' containing a 'span.cell-text' label node -- Zoplicate queries both selectors to inject a duplicate-count badge. | The cell element accepts appended child span nodes (Zoplicate adds a '.number' badge) without renderItem overwriting them on re-render of unchanged rows.

Verify collection-tree-row-duplicates-search-object (radar) contracts still hold on the release tier: On a duplicates row, getSearchObject() delegates to this.ref.getSearchObject() (Zotero.Duplicates.getSearchObject) for the row's library. | The duplicate temp table is dropped on row unload (this.onUnload), the lifecycle Zoplicate's caching compensates for. | isDuplicates() and .ref.libraryID remain the row's stable identity surface that Zoplicate reads.

Verify item-save-data (radar) contracts still hold on the release tier: _saveData(env) receives env.isNew, env.options.skipNotifier, env.options.notifierQueue, and a mutable env.notifierData that is forwarded to Zotero.Notifier.queue('modify'/'add', 'item', id, env.notifierData) -- Zoplicate injects env.notifierData.refreshDuplicates. | this._changed.creators and this._changed.itemData expose which fields changed before save; Zoplicate gates the duplicate-refresh flag on them plus this.isRegularItem(). | this.parentID is readable/writable inside _saveData before original.call, so Zoplicate can re-parent a child whose parent was trashed (used with DuplicateFinder/DuplicateItems master selection).

Verify merge-items (radar) contracts still hold on the release tier: mergeItems(items, ...) merges a set of duplicate items into a master, handling relations, attachments, and trashing of non-masters. | Calling mergeItems is sufficient for Zoplicate's bulk merge; Zoplicate does not reimplement merge side effects.
Do NOT modify release-targeting product code; the released/beta versions are unaffected.
For each dev-only change, trace where the relocated logic went (one hop) and record new watch targets.
Run uv run python scripts/ci/check_zotero_upstream.py --check-only to refresh radar status.
